title: Tanker Shipping - July 14, 2026
summary: Geopolitical tensions between the United States and Iran have disrupted the recovery of tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, leading to renewed caution among shipowners. Rising oil prices due to transit risks, coupled with efforts by Gulf producers to expand export infrastructure outside the Strait, are driving market attention. The tanker market is facing a potential downcycle due to a significant build-up in new vessel orders.
